design_philosophy.core_loop: Select player → Execute pass/shoot/defensive action → Progress ball toward goal → Score or defend against opponent
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time positional management and timing of passes/shots against computer or human opposition
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Manage and play as a national football team in competitive international matches (World Cup format, league play, exhibition)
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Uses isometric viewpoint (deliberate differentiation from top-down competitors like Sensible Soccer and Kick Off); emphasizes visual animation and presentation detail over gameplay innovation; positions itself as 'more realistic' alternative to existing football games

difficulty.checkpointing: Match-based (each match is discrete; tournament/league progress persists across sessions)
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player selects team and game mode; no explicit difficulty settings documented; computer AI opponent difficulty not specified

progression.unlock_mechanism: Tournament and Playoffs modes progress through group stage → knockout format (4 matches possible after initial 3 group games); League mode is double round-robin with 8 teams
replayability_hooks: Multiple game modes (Exhibition, Tournament, Playoffs, League)
Ability to control players on either team

world_building_techniques: National team identity via flag representation and country names
Commentary track (PC CD-ROM version only, by Tony Gubba)

faction_indicators: 48 playable national teams representing AFC, CAF, CONCACAF, CONMEBOL, OFC, UEFA confederations
International match structure (group stage → knockout progression mirrors real FIFA World Cup format)
environmental_storytelling: Isometric stadium viewpoint establishes football field as primary play space
Crowd sound effects and player animation detail create match atmosphere
